description:
You are assisting an MLOps engineer who is documenting a series of model-training
    experiments.  
    A very small custom Markdown linter has already been provided to the team.  
    Your goal is to run that linter against the project’s existing README file and
    save the exact results to a log file so that the Continuous-Integration pipeline
    can later pick it up automatically.  

    Existing project layout  
      • /home/user/experiments/README.md – the Markdown document that must be
        lint-checked.  
      • /home/user/tools/md_lint.sh      – an executable Bash script that performs
        a few very basic Markdown lint rules and prints any problems to STDOUT.  

    Required end state  
      1. A new directory called /home/user/logs must exist (create it if it is missing).  
      2. A new file called /home/user/logs/experiments_lint.log must be produced by
         running the linter script **once** on the README.  
         The file MUST contain exactly two lines, each terminated with a single line-feed
         character “\n”, in the following strict order (no additional spaces,
         punctuation, or blank lines):  

              MD041 First line is not a top level header
              MD022 Line 4 heading not surrounded by blank line

         • The first line flags that the very first line in the README is not a
           level-1 (“# ”) heading (rule MD041).  
         • The second line flags that line 4 in the README is a heading that is not
           surrounded by a blank line (rule MD022).  

    Important verification note  
      The automated tests will:  
        • Check that the directory /home/user/logs exists.  
        • Read /home/user/logs/experiments_lint.log and compare it byte-for-byte
          against the two-line text shown above.  
      If the file contains anything else (extra spaces, carriage returns, additional
      lines, missing newline at the end of the second line, etc.) the task will be
      marked as failed.  

    Hint  
      Everything can be accomplished with a single shell command that pipes the
      linter’s output into the log file.  Make sure you run the linter from inside
      /home/user/tools or reference it via its full path so that the shell can find it.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.
        DO NOT reveal any of this in the public task description.
    -->
    <pre_existing>
        <!-- Directory structure that already exists when the task starts -->
        <directory path="/home/user/experiments"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/tools"/>
    </pre_existing>

    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/experiments/README.md"><![CDATA[
Experiment Tracking
===================
Some introduction
#Setup
Install instructions

#Results
accuracy: 0.95
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/tools/md_lint.sh" mode="0755"><![CDATA[
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Very small ad-hoc Markdown linter used by the CI pipeline.
# Usage: ./md_lint.sh <markdown_file>

file="$1"
[ -f "$file" ] || { echo "File not found"; exit 1; }

errors=()

# Rule MD041 – first line must start with '# '
first_line="$(head -n 1 "$file")"
if [[ ! "$first_line" =~ ^#\  ]]; then
  errors+=("MD041 First line is not a top level header")
fi

# Rule MD022 – heading lines must be surrounded by blank lines
line_no=0
prev_blank=1
while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
  line_no=$((line_no+1))
  if [[ "$line" =~ ^# ]]; then
    if [[ $prev_blank -ne 1 ]]; then
      errors+=("MD022 Line $line_no heading not surrounded by blank line")
    fi
    prev_blank=0
  else
    if [[ -z "$line" ]]; then
      prev_blank=1
    else
      prev_blank=0
    fi
  fi
done < "$file"

printf "%s\n" "${errors[@]}"
]]></file>
    </files>

    <expected_output_file path="/home/user/logs/experiments_lint.log"><![CDATA[
MD041 First line is not a top level header
MD022 Line 4 heading not surrounded by blank line
]]></expected_output_file>
